title: Crunchy Argentinian Beef Stir-fry
description: A savory, crunchy stirfry featuring thin beef cutlets, bell pepper, and a tangy Argentinian-inspired sauce, all glutenfree.
instructions: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium‑high heat and add 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
When hot, add the sliced beef cutlet in a single layer, letting it sear for 1‑2 minutes per side until browned but not fully cooked; remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil, then toss in the sliced onion and bell pepper, stir‑frying for 3‑4 minutes until slightly softened.
Add minced garlic, cumin, paprika, and a pinch of salt; cook for another 30 seconds, letting the spices bloom.
Return the browned beef to the skillet and stir to combine, cooking another 1‑2 minutes until the beef is just cooked through.
Stir in the lime juice and chopped cilantro, adjusting salt to taste.
Remove from heat and sprinkle the crushed corn tortilla chips over the top, tossing gently to coat the stir‑fry with the crunchy chips.
Serve immediately over a bed of fresh greens or as a standalone dish.
Optionally garnish with extra cilantro or a squeeze of lime.
Enjoy the salty, crunchy Argentinian‑inspired beef stir‑fry!
